description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle with a natural wood grain texture and a smooth finish. The handle is ergonomically shaped for a comfortable grip, tapering slightly towards the end. The axe head is made of metal, with a broad, wedge-shaped blade and a flat poll on the opposite side, suitable for splitting or chopping wood. The blade is securely mounted onto the handle, with the mounting area showing a traditional wedged fit. The metal head has a matte, slightly rough surface, and the overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements or branding. The object appears well-suited for use in urban environments such as parks, garages, or maintenance areas.
description: A hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a broad metal blade, featuring a traditional wedged mounting and a utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the narrow edge of the blade and the top of the handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full profile of the curved wooden handle and the broad metal blade.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the flat poll of the axe head and the straight alignment of the handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other profile of the handle and blade, with visible wood grain and blade geometry.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a smooth, ergonomic grip and oval cross-section, natural wood grain visible.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad wedge-shaped steel blade with a flat poll, no beard, and a straight cutting edge.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged fit with the blade mounted onto the handle through the eye.
